mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and housing demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with accuracy greater than 55% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 65% of observations during expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds the 300 threshold, elevated inflation expectations trigger anticipated monetary tightening and higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 300.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly contractions in real GDP and retail sales confirm a broad-based demand shock, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: The combined decline of GDPC1 and RSAFS predicts positive returns in greater than 55% of cases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a decelerating industrial production index signals peak capacity utilization and impending supply bottlenecks, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Decelerating INDPRO yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 60% of observations during late-cycle regimes.
